evals.description: Trigger test: activates on REST endpoint testing question
evals.prompt: I have an Express API and I want to write tests for my GET /products/:id endpoint. What's the best way to test it without spinning up a real server?

evals.description: Trigger test: activates on contract testing question
evals.prompt: We have a checkout service that calls an order service. We keep breaking the integration when either team deploys. What is contract testing and how would Pact help here?

evals.description: Trigger test: activates on MSW / mock server question
evals.prompt: My frontend app calls three different external APIs and I want to write integration tests without hitting real services. Should I use MSW?

evals.description: Core task: write Supertest integration test for a POST endpoint
evals.prompt: Write an integration test using Supertest for a POST /orders endpoint that creates an order. It should test the successful case and the case where required fields are missing.

evals.description: Core task: validate API response schema with Zod
evals.prompt: How do I use Zod to validate that my API response matches the expected shape in my tests? I want a single assertion that catches any structural regression.

evals.description: Core task: test all authentication states for a protected endpoint
evals.prompt: What authentication cases should I test for a JWT-protected endpoint, and can you show me the test structure?

evals.description: Core task: set up Pact consumer test
evals.prompt: Show me how to write a Pact consumer test for a user service that my app calls to fetch user profiles. I want to define what response shape I expect.

evals.description: Core task: configure MSW server for Node.js tests
evals.prompt: How do I configure MSW in a Jest/Node test environment so it intercepts all HTTP calls? I want it to error on any unhandled requests.

evals.description: Core task: test GraphQL query with Apollo Server
evals.prompt: I have an Apollo Server GraphQL API. How do I write tests for a query without making real HTTP requests?

evals.description: Gotcha: warns about not leaking stack traces in error responses
evals.prompt: I'm testing my error handling and I want to make sure my API never exposes internal details like stack traces in 500 responses. How do I assert for this?

evals.description: Gotcha: warns about shared state causing flaky tests
evals.prompt: My API tests pass individually but fail when I run the whole suite. Some tests seem to be interfering with each other. What is likely going wrong?

evals.description: Anti-hallucination: recommends testing error shapes, not just status codes
evals.prompt: Is it enough to just assert the status code in my API tests, like expect(res.status).toBe(422)?
